title: Application Deployment Files
breadcrumbs: Deployment Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Application Deployment Files Application files can reside on servers and on clients. Uniface deployment files can include: UAR (Uniface Archive) files—ZIP-compressed file containing components, objects, and descriptors in a fixed directory structure Resource files—components, other compiled objects, and various files Deployment files can reside on servers and on clients. For example, forms that are used by multiple clients are usually located on a central server and services are often located on fast servers rather than on clients. If you are activating services remotely, you must ensure that up-to-date signatures are available (in UAR files) on the servers on which the services are activated. Component files can be deployed on the file system or in UAR files. Files that are frequently used should be placed locally. This may be worth considering for forms and services. Although this duplicates files in multiple locations, it can improve performance. Related Links UAR Files A Uniface Deployment Archive (UAR) file is a zip file with a .uar extension that contains the application's runtime objects in a standardized directory structure. Standardized Directory Structure Runtime objects are always stored in a fixed directory structure, whether that is in a compilation output directory or a deployed Uniface archive file.
